The objection raised against the
adoption of the commission plan
on this account also holds good
against our present system of
municipal government.


Objection has been found to the
Des Moines plan by representatives
of the Direct Legislation league OF
Spokane on the ground that the
25 per cent of the voters required
for the submission of a matter to
the people under the Des Moines
plan is too large and that the
number should be 10 or 10 per
cent. This matter can easily be
adjusted by following the prece-
dent of the state In the number OF
signatures required for the sub
mission of an amendment to the
city charter, which is, as above
stated, only 10 per cent.


The enactment of 1903 covering
the submission to the people of an
amendment to the city charter
reads:


"On petition of a number equal
to Is per cent of the total number
of votes cast at the last preceding
municipal election) of qualified
voters of any municipality having
adopted a charter under the laws
of this state, asking the adoption
of a specified charter amendment
providing for any matter within
the realm of local affairs, or mu
nicipal business, the said amend
ment shall be submitted to the
voters at the next regular NUM-
cipal election, occurring 80 days or
more after said petition is filed,
and II approved by majority OF
the local electors of the municipal
ity voting upon 1t, such amend
ment shall become a part of the
charter organic law governing such
municipality.


THE petition containing the de-
mand for the submission of the
proposed charter amendment shall
be filed with the city clerk and
each signer shall write his occupa-
ton and residence after his sigma
ture. and the genuineness of the
signatures on such paper must be
attested by the affidavit of a quail
